title: `orca assign` race: send keys before freshly-spawned amux pane settles (~18% failure)
description:
orca assign fails ~18% of the time (41/229 in 2 weeks of Claude/Codex traces). Concrete errors:
- 'assign rpc failed: prompt delivery failed after 3 attempts' (most common)
- 'amux send-keys: pane "w-LAB-xxxx" not found'
- 'amux ... window "alphaosZ" not found'

ROOT CAUSE: a race between amux pane/window spawn and the first SendKeys in the assignment path. amux already exposes the right primitive — WaitIdle/WaitIdleSettle/WaitContent (internal/amux/client.go). client.Spawn uses WaitIdle correctly (~client.go:155, after cd+Enter). But the assignment prompt-delivery path (internal/daemon/helpers.go sendKeysToLivePane -> assign_startup.go sendAssignmentStartupPrompt) does only ensureLivePane and sends keys BEFORE the freshly-spawned pane has settled, so amux hasn't registered the pane/window yet. The codex handshake path (prompt_delivery.go:~86) does call WaitIdle, but only after baseline capture, not immediately post-spawn.

FIX: add an amux.WaitIdle (or readiness check) right after spawnWorkerPane returns and before the first SendKeys in the assignment startup, mirroring client.Spawn. Existing primitive, no new logic. Make window creation idempotent so a missing target window is created rather than erroring.

Evidence source: trace-mining in weill-labs/reflection (issue reflection-e80).
acceptance_criteria: Assignment startup waits for pane readiness (amux.WaitIdle) before the first SendKeys; target window is created idempotently; assign failure rate from 'pane/window not found' and 'prompt delivery failed' drops materially.

title: Phase 4: Complete-on-merge -> auto-unblock

title: Phase 2: BeadsSource (shells bd --json) + Verify

title: Phase 3: idle-pull dispatch loop (opt-in, default OFF)

title: Adopt beads as a worksource in orca
description: Pull-based dependency-aware dispatch via a worksource.Source interface + BeadsSource. orca stays a thin executor; Linear stays system of record. Plan + status: Linear LAB-1922.

title: spawner-comms: implement worker->lieutenant notify-pane convention (LAB-1956)
description:
Implement worker->lieutenant comms per the merged spec docs/specs/worker-lieutenant-comms.md (LAB-1946 design spike). Prompt plumbing only — no daemon state changes, no FSM changes.

Settled decisions (from the spec table):
- Channel: raw `amux send-keys` (defer structured channel).
- Ack model: fire-and-forget (defer ask-and-wait).
- Autonomous-loop with no notify pane: omit the convention.
- Profile vs per-assign: per-assign override + repo-config default; agent profiles unchanged.

Resolution chain (apply in this order):
  AssignRequest.NotifyPane (--notify-pane flag) ->
  callerPane (manual AMUX_PANE, already plumbed in d.assign) ->
  repo config [notifications] notification_pane ->
  "" (omit the convention).

Inject EXACTLY this text into the prompt when a pane resolves (do NOT invent your own phrasing — this is the spec convention verbatim):

  To notify or ask the lieutenant, run `amux send-keys <PANE> "<one-line message>"`.
  Use it for: a blocking question before you guess, or a milestone (PR opened).
  Keep messages to one line; do not spam.

Substitute <PANE> with the resolved pane id.

Files to touch:
- internal/daemon/assign_prompt.go: add `appendNotifyConvention(prompt, pane string) string`; idempotent (do not double-append if convention already present).
- internal/daemon/assign.go: compute resolved pane via the chain above, call the appender after wrapAssignmentPrompt.
- internal/daemon/controller.go: AssignRequest.NotifyPane string `json:"notify_pane,omitempty"`.
- internal/daemon/worksource_config.go (or split into loadRepoConfig): extend rawRepoConfig with `[notifications] notification_pane`; surface as cfg.NotificationPane. Default empty.
- internal/cli/app.go: `--notify-pane PANE` flag on `orca assign`; plumb into AssignRequest; update the usage string and command help.
- internal/daemon/pull.go: no code change required (callerPane is already empty in the autonomous path; resolution naturally falls through to config or omit).
- docs/specs/orca-design.md: update the "Notifications" section — flip the prior "do not use send-keys" caution to reference the alphazero evidence + this feature; cross-link worker-lieutenant-comms.md.
- docs/specs/worker-lieutenant-comms.md: update the Status line at top from "design spike (no implementation yet)" to "implemented in PR #N" (fill in N once the PR is open).

Tests (table-driven, t.Parallel in each subtest; run new tests with -count=100):
- Prompt-injection unit test: convention appended when pane resolves; NOT appended when pane is empty; idempotent (calling appender twice produces same result).
- Resolution-chain unit test: covers all four positions (--notify-pane wins; falls to callerPane; falls to config; otherwise empty).
- Config loading test: reads [notifications] notification_pane from .orca/config.toml; absent section -> empty.
- CLI flag test: `orca assign --notify-pane pane-13 ...` plumbs the value into AssignRequest.
- Update existing tests for wrapAssignmentPrompt / assign_parallel_prompt_race that include the prompt verbatim, so they still pass.

Scope guards:
- Touch ONLY the files listed above plus their tests. Do NOT modify internal/amux, internal/worksource, internal/state, or any unrelated area. Files under 500 LOC; patch coverage >= 80%.
- Use the EXACT convention text above verbatim — do not paraphrase or restructure.
- Keep AgentProfile unchanged; no profile-level convention default in this PR.
- The autonomous-loop path (no callerPane, no config) must omit the convention silently (no warning log spam every 30s).

Branch off origin/main. Open a PR titled with this issue id. Iterate until CI is green.

title: worksource: Complete handles both bd close shapes + skip when no beads id
description:
Finish the worksource id/shape consolidation (the A+C id-resolver already exists in internal/daemon/worksource_ids.go). Two remaining seams, both currently NON-FATAL but noisy:

1. Bug D (response shape): 'bd close <id> --suggest-next --json' returns the {closed,unblocked} OBJECT when the close unblocks dependents, but a bare ARRAY (the closed issues) when it unblocks NONE (the common leaf-task case). The worksource Complete path (internal/worksource/beads_source.go, type beadsCloseResult) only decodes the object, so leaf completions fail with 'cannot unmarshal array into ... beadsCloseResult' and orca logs 'worksource complete failed' — note the bd close subprocess SUCCEEDS first (the bead closes), so only the response-parse and the unblocked-set are lost. Fix: decode BOTH shapes — if the JSON top-level is an array, treat it as the closed set with empty unblocked; if it is the object, use closed+unblocked as today.

2. Clean-skip on no-mapping: beadsIDForCompletion (internal/daemon/worksource_ids.go) currently falls back to the raw task id when no beads id resolves, so Complete then runs 'bd close <linear-id>' for a Linear-id task that has no matching bead (e.g. amux LAB-#### tasks) -> fails non-fatally and logs noise. Fix: when no beads id can be resolved AND the task id is not itself a beads id, SKIP the worksource close entirely (do not attempt bd close with a non-beads id); emit a concise skip (like the existing status-skip event) instead of a failure.

Keep everything NON-FATAL (never break completion). Do NOT delete internal/linear and do NOT switch Linear to push/cron — real-time Linear write-back stays.

Tests (table-driven, call t.Parallel in each subtest, run new tests with -count=100): Complete parses BOTH the array shape and the {closed,unblocked} object shape of bd close --suggest-next; a no-beads-id Linear task SKIPS the close cleanly (no bd close attempted, a skip logged, no 'complete failed'); a resolvable beads id still closes correctly.

SCOPE: touch ONLY internal/worksource/beads_source.go (close-response decode), internal/daemon/worksource_ids.go (clean-skip), the Complete call site (postmortem.go and/or pull.go), and their tests. Do NOT touch internal/amux or unrelated code. Files under 500 lines; patch coverage at least 80 percent. Branch off origin/main. Open a PR titled with this issue id. Iterate until CI is green.

title: worksource: resolve task id <-> beads/Linear id (fix A+C)
description:
Fix the beads-id vs Linear-id duality behind Bugs A and C (found during worksource loop validation). orca's task id is sometimes a BEADS id (the autonomous pull loop dispatches WorkItem.ID) and sometimes a LINEAR id (manual 'orca assign LAB-####'). The worksource integration assumes one, causing two seams:
- Bug A: Linear status write-back (setIssueStatus in internal/daemon/issue_status.go) is called with the task id; for a beads id it is not in Linear, so status is skipped (already non-fatal via isLinearEntityNotFoundError) but the linked Linear issue never gets updated.
- Bug C: the worksource Complete path (Phase 4) runs 'bd close <task-id>'; for a LINEAR id it is not a beads id, so 'bd close' fails (logged, non-fatal), so manual-assign beads issues never auto-close.

Implement an id-resolution helper that maps a given orca task id to its (beads_id, linear_id) pair:
- If the task id is a beads id: 'bd show <id> --json' returns its external_ref (the Linear id).
- If the task id is a Linear id (e.g. LAB-1234): find the bead whose external_ref matches via 'bd search --external-contains <linear-id> --json' (this filter exists), then use that bead's id.
Route each op to the right id:
- Linear status write-back: use the LINEAR id (the task id if already Linear, else the resolved external_ref); keep the existing non-fatal skip when there is no Linear id.
- worksource Complete / 'bd close': use the BEADS id (the task id if already a bead, else the reverse-looked-up bead); keep non-fatal/log when there is no beads id.
- BOTH paths MUST remain NON-FATAL: never break assign or completion if a mapping is missing or bd errors.

Tests (table-driven, t.Parallel in each subtest, run new tests with -count=100): beads-id task -> status uses external_ref Linear id AND close uses the beads id; Linear-id task -> status uses the Linear id AND close uses the reverse-looked-up beads id; missing-mapping -> graceful skip.

SCOPE: touch ONLY the worksource id-resolution + status/Complete call sites (internal/daemon: issue_status.go, helpers.go, the Complete path in pull.go and/or postmortem.go, internal/worksource if a shared helper belongs there). Do NOT refactor unrelated code or other packages (e.g. internal/amux). Files under 500 lines; patch coverage at least 80 percent. Branch off origin/main. Open a PR titled with this issue id. Iterate until CI is green.

title: worksource pull tick: emit per-tick observability trace
description: Add observability to the worksource pull tick. In internal/daemon/pull.go, a healthy tick (runPullTick / pullReadyWork) currently logs only on error, so idle and active ticks are invisible. Emit ONE concise log line per tick via the daemon's logf (match the style of the other daemon tick log lines) summarizing: free clone count, number of ready items, number dispatched, and number skipped due to worksource.ErrAlreadyClaimed. Do NOT log when the loop is disabled (workSourceEnabled false). Add a table-driven unit test with t.Parallel. Keep files under 500 lines; patch coverage at least 80 percent. Branch off origin/main. Open a PR titled with this issue id. Iterate until CI is green.

description: Optional profile nudge template; never auto-kills.

title: PM-2 (post-MVP): auto-rebalance before escalate
description: Release claim -> pull loop re-dispatches to free worker; design double-work race first; never auto-kills.

title: Phase 5 (deferred): delete internal/linear; beads owns Linear
description: Collapse issueTracker into worksource; enable bd linear --push/cron. Sign-off-gated (trades real-time Linear status for cron-lag).
